name: The Ocean Club, A Four Seasons Resort

content.verdict: The Ocean Club is the legendary Paradise Island resort — originally Huntington Hartford's private retreat, the setting for the Bond film Casino Royale, and now a Four Seasons-managed icon with 35 acres of formal Versailles Gardens, a five-mile stretch of white-sand beach, and an aristocratic atmosphere unlike anywhere else in the Caribbean. For honeymooners willing to splurge, it remains the gold standard for Bahamas luxury.
content.best_room: The Hartford Wing rooms and suites are closest to the beach and the formal cloister — they have the most direct ocean access and the property's most desirable position. Crescent Wing rooms are larger and more contemporary with private balconies overlooking Cabbage Beach. Honeymooners should request a Hartford Junior Suite ocean-front for the perfect combination of historic atmosphere and direct beach access. The standalone villas with private pools are the ultimate splurge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Versailles Gardens
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Nassau and cross the Paradise Island bridge — 25 minutes total. Check in at the historic main building, then walk the Versailles Gardens to the 12th-century French cloister that Hartford imported and reassembled stone by stone. First dinner at Dune, Jean-Georges Vongerichten's beachfront restaurant, watching the moon rise over Cabbage Beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cabbage Beach & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Ocean Club has direct access to the longest stretch of Cabbage Beach. Morning swim, then a couples treatment at the Balinese-style spa villas tucked into tropical gardens. Lunch at the Pool Café, afternoon at the adults-only quiet pool, sunset cocktails at the Martini Bar — a bar with over 100 martini variations.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Atlantis Aquarium
itinerary_7_nights.description: The neighboring Atlantis resort is a 10-minute walk down the beach — Ocean Club guests get easy access. Spend half a day at The Dig aquarium (50,000 marine animals across 20 million gallons), the Predator Lagoon shark tank, and the Marine Habitat. Lunch at Nobu Atlantis. Return to the Ocean Club for the calmer evening atmosphere — the contrast between the two properties is part of the experience.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Exumas Powerboat Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day powerboat charter to the Exumas — Pig Beach on Big Major Cay where the famous swimming pigs paddle out to the boat, Compass Cay's friendly nurse sharks, Thunderball Grotto from the James Bond film, and a private sandbar lunch. The 90-minute open-water crossing is dramatic but the Exumas waters are some of the most beautiful in the world.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Tennis, Golf or Sailing
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Ocean Club has nine tennis courts (Hartford was a serious player) and arrangements with the Ocean Club Golf Course, a Tom Weiskopf design on Paradise Island. Afternoon Hobie Cat sailing or paddleboarding off Cabbage Beach. Dinner at Dune again — this time at a beach-edge table with the sound of the surf.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Downtown Nassau & Junkanoo
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning trip to downtown Nassau — the Straw Market, Bay Street's colonial pink Government House, and the Pirates of Nassau Museum. Lunch at Graycliff for traditional Bahamian conch chowder. If your visit aligns with a Friday or Saturday night, time the return for live Junkanoo drumming on Bay Street. Evening back at the Ocean Club for a private beach dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Beach Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A morning swim on Cabbage Beach, breakfast at the Hartford Wing terrace, and a final walk through the Versailles Gardens. Checkout is leisurely — the Four Seasons manages departure with practiced grace. Nassau's airport is 25 minutes away.

caveats: The Ocean Club is on Paradise Island, immediately next to the enormous Atlantis resort — Cabbage Beach is shared and gets busy, particularly with day visitors and cruise passengers in peak season.
Pricing is at the very top of the Bahamas market; even by Four Seasons standards, the Ocean Club is exceptional in cost. Suites and villas can exceed $5,000 per night in peak season.

While the property feels secluded inside its gates, the broader Paradise Island environment is heavily developed with casinos and chain resorts — couples seeking unspoiled remote settings should look at the Out Islands.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: The Ocean Club is one of the world's classic honeymoon hotels — the gardens, the cloister, the historic atmosphere, and the gold-standard Four Seasons service create one of the most romantic environments in the Caribbean. The aristocratic feel, the beachfront dining, and the spa villas make it ideal for couples wanting traditional luxury rather than barefoot eco-style.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is peak season with consistently dry, warm days and cool evenings. November and May offer better value with similar weather. Hurricane season is August through October — most travelers avoid these months despite reduced rates.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: The Ocean Club is one of the Caribbean's hardest-to-book honeymoon hotels. Hartford Wing rooms book 6 to 9 months out for high season. Christmas, New Year, and Easter sell out almost a year in advance. The villas with private pools should be booked as early as possible.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: The Ocean Club is not adults-only and welcomes families, but its formal architecture, garden setting, and price point mean the atmosphere skews adult and especially honeymoon-heavy. The adults quiet pool gives couples a dedicated space.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: The Hartford Junior Suite ocean-front offers the property's most romantic position — direct beach access and the historic main building. The Crescent Wing rooms are more contemporary and slightly larger. The standalone villas with private pools are the ultimate splurge for couples wanting total privacy.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Lynden Pindling International Airport (NAS) is 25 minutes via the Paradise Island bridge. Direct flights from Miami, New York, Atlanta, and London make Nassau one of the most accessible Caribbean destinations. The Ocean Club arranges luxury transfers in Mercedes vehicles.
